violations.text: An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026
Inspector observed beverage tubes draining into the hand sink in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice used for drinks. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The following were observed in poor repair: 1) The wall in the mop sink area is in poor repair, inspector observed exposed wood. 2) The wall in the ware washing area is in poor repair, inspector observed chipped paint. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the refuse area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Food was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and covered with plastic wrap. Food was relocated to a sheet pan to rapidly cool. Proper cooling methods were reviewed with the person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Knives are stored on an unclean surface (between the prep table and wall) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. An employee illness policy was given to the person in charge during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Food was observed held at 44-55 degrees Fahrenheit in the low boy unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge food had been in the low boy unit for more than 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Low boy had an ambient air temperature of 41-43 degrees Fahrenheit. Low boy was taken out of service during time of inspection and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Per person in charge, low boy unit will be serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023
